meaning: n.手铐,脚镣,桎梏,束缚物vt.加桎梏,加枷锁,束缚
examples: Superstition is a great shackle on men's minds.
But debt can also limit one's freedom-it's a shackle, a contract.
He says Gerrard would be an essential signing, while urging the club to shackle Maicon.
Whether electorates will be quite so quick to shackle themselves to Germanic fiscal rules is another matter.
But an unregulated environment attracts all those who do not want to play by the rules that shackle traditional money managers.
